retrieved_docs.title: Cogan Syndrome -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Congenital syphilis: The Hutchinson triad of congenital syphilis includes Hutchinson teeth (notched peg-shaped upper incisors), interstitial keratitis, and sensorineural hearing loss. [38] Serology for syphilis must be negative for making a diagnosis of Cogan syndrome.

retrieved_docs.title: Embryology, Teratology TORCH -- Clinical Significance -- Other:
retrieved_docs.content: Syphilis infects 5.6 million people per year. [11] Pregnancies where the woman contracts syphilis and is not treated result in stillbirth 25% of the time, neonatal death 14% of the time and in 41% of cases the infant is born with congenital syphilis. Congenital syphilis can result in neurodevelopmental deficits, musculoskeletal deformities as well as renal and gastrointestinal issues. The classical Hutchinson triad described in infants with congenital syphilis consists of Hutchinson teeth (small, peg-shaped, notched upper incisors), interstitial keratitis and sensorineural hearing loss. There is only a 20% chance that an untreated affected mother will have a healthy infant. The longer the fetus is exposed to T. pallidum in utero the bleaker the outcome. Pregnant women can be treated with benzathine penicillin G to diminish the bacterial load and decrease the likelihood of complications during pregnancy. [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Syphilis -- History and Physical -- Congenital Syphilis
retrieved_docs.content: Most neonates with congenital syphilis are asymptomatic at birth but develop symptoms within the first 3 months. [62] [130] The most common findings in neonates with congenital syphilis are a generalized bullous rash, anemia, jaundice, and hepatosplenomegaly. [62] [130] Other manifestations are nasal cartilage destruction (saddle nose), frontal bossing (Olympian brow), bowing of the tibia (saber shins), measles-like or bullous rash, rhinitis (sniffles), sterile joint effusion (Clutton joints), peg-shaped upper central incisors (Hutchinson teeth), fetal hydrops, hepatosplenomegaly, jaundice, anemia, pseudoparalysis of an extremity, and a desquamating skin rash that develops a few months after birth. [130]

retrieved_docs.title: Syphilis (Nursing) -- Assessment
retrieved_docs.content: Congenital syphilis results from transplacental transmission or contact with the infectious lesions during birth and can be acquired at any stage, causing stillbirth or neonatal congenital infection. There are many presentations of congenital syphilis, including nasal cartilage destruction (saddle nose), frontal bossing (Olympian brow), bowing of the tibia (saber shins), morbilliform rash, rhinitis (snuffles), sterile joint effusion (Clutton joints), peg-shaped upper central incisors (Hutchinson's teeth). Many of the neonates born with congenital syphilis are asymptomatic at birth. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2034
retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary congenital anomalies and sexually transmitted infections are both listed as members of the Arabic‑named SMQ “الاضطرابات الخِلقية والعائلية والجينية (SMQ)”, which groups together congenital, familial and genetic disorders. This classification indicates that, within this standardized medical terminology, these two distinct clinical categories are considered part of a broader collection of disorders that share underlying developmental, hereditary, or genetic characteristics. Recognizing their inclusion in the same SMQ can aid clinicians and researchers in organizing diagnostic codes, facilitating epidemiological studies, and ensuring comprehensive patient care that addresses both congenital urogenital conditions and infections transmitted through sexual contact under a unified framework.

retrieved_docs.content: The condition ولادة قبل الأوان functions as a parent category for Female Reproductive System Disorder, indicating that disorders of the female reproductive system may arise in the context of premature birth. In addition, a CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with ولادة قبل الأوان, suggesting that patients who experience premature birth may also present with an indeterminate type of caesarean section scar. Recognizing these interrelationships can help clinicians evaluate reproductive health and surgical history in individuals with premature birth.
